author: Donald Barry Smith Jr
executive_summary: Donald Barry Smith Jr opposes the proposed rule change, arguing that the existing rule is functioning adequately and that the proposed modification is motivated by Wall Street profit-seeking rather than genuine market improvement. He warns that removing the rule would harm retail investors by allowing trading firms to route orders opportunistically for their own financial benefit.
market_structure_topics: Conflicts of interest in order flow

positions.order_competition_rule: Strongly opposes any removal or weakening of the existing rule, asserting it is working as intended
positions.best_execution: Implicitly supports best execution protections, warning that deregulation would undermine fair order routing for retail investors
positions.transparency: Not explicitly addressed, though concern about firm discretion in order routing implies support for transparency constraints

economic_arguments: The proposed rule change is characterized as a mechanism to increase profits for Wall Street trading firms rather than improve market efficiency
Removing the rule would shift economic benefits away from retail investors toward institutional trading firms
competition_arguments: Warns that without the rule, trading firms would selectively route orders to venues that maximize their own profits, undermining competitive and neutral order routing
retail_investor_arguments: Retail investors will bear significant costs if the rule is removed
The current rule provides meaningful protection to retail participants
Deregulation in this area disproportionately benefits sophisticated trading firms at the expense of ordinary investors
recommended_actions: Retain the existing rule without modification
Reject the justification that the current rule creates undue complexity
key_quotes: This is just a way for wall street to make more money.
The rule is working just fine.
The justification that it is 'making trading too complex' is laughable.
Removing this rule will allow trading firms to pick and choose where to send orders in order to increase profits.
Retail investors will pay a heavy price if this rule is removed.
